tagline: Achiote-pineapple marinated chicken, charred corn salsa, fresh crema-style cool-down on crispy corn tortillas.
ingredients: chicken al pastor (LTO — pineapple, achiote, chipotle)
signal_rank_note: Al pastor is a strong but not top-tier signal in the Mission (0.58) — mole, pesto, and miso all outrank it citywide, but al pastor is the honest play given a Mission burrito crowd that will judge an al pastor build against El Farolito two blocks away.
novelty_check: Chipotle's standard al pastor is sold as a burrito/bowl protein swap; positioning it as a taco plate with corn salsa + crema-style sour cream + guac as a composed three-taco set (not a build-your-own) is a distinct LTO format, not a near-duplicate of the assembly-line default.
confidence_reason: Al pastor has been shipped in 2023, 2024, and 2026 — active pantry, proven supply chain, and the Mission's taqueria density means the audience already searches for and orders this flavor regularly.

dish_potential: A Mole Chicken Bowl / Mole Burrito line — mole as a finishing sauce ladled over adobo chicken or carnitas with black beans, cilantro-lime rice, queso blanco, and pickled onions (if added). Opens a fall/Día de los Muertos LTO window and gives Chipotle a credible answer to the Mission's mole-forward Oaxacan spots.
operational_lift: Medium lift — mole ships as a shelf-stable paste or refrigerated pouch (similar SKU profile to existing salsas) and slots into the salsa/queso ladle station without new equipment.
comp_context: Nopalito and La Urbana in/near the Mission have built loyal followings on mole negro and mole rojo plates; Californios elevates it at the high end. Mission diners already have a calibrated mole palate that Chipotle currently cannot meet.

tagline: Black lentils, harissa honey chicken, sumac slaw, and lemon herb tahini over saffron rice.
signal_rank_note: Lentils is a notable but not top-tier signal in the Mission (0.33) — Mediterranean/Levantine flavors index lower here than Mexican or East Asian, but this build leans into CAVA's strongest active LTOs (sumac slaw + sumac chips landed 2026) and stacks three on-pantry signals (lentils, tahini, harissa) inside one cuisine-coherent bowl.
novelty_check: Distinct from a generic build-your-own — it's a composed signature pairing harissa honey chicken with black lentils as a co-base (not a protein swap), with the 2026 sumac slaw as the named crunch element.
confidence_reason: All ingredients are active pantry; sumac slaw was shipped in 2026 so relaunch timing intelligence is current. Signal scores are modest in this neighborhood, so this is a loyalist-pleaser rather than a foot-traffic driver.

dish_potential: Roasted cauliflower as a standalone vegetable topping (currently bundled inside the 'roasted vegetables' mix) unlocks a Harissa Cauliflower Bowl, a Cauliflower + Tahini mezze plate, and a vegetarian hero that competes with Souvla and the Mission's plant-forward Mediterranean spots. Pairs cleanly with existing harissa, tahini, and sumac SKUs — all compatible co-flavors.
operational_lift: Low lift — CAVA already roasts cauliflower as part of the roasted vegetables blend; separating it into its own pan is a prep-line change, not new equipment or supply chain.
comp_context: Beit Rima and Tawla (Mission/nearby) have turned roasted/charred cauliflower with tahini and sumac into signature plates; the dish is a known winner with this exact customer.

tagline: Miso glazed salmon, crispy rice, nori sesame, napa cabbage slaw, and miso sesame ginger over wild rice.
signal_rank_note: Miso is a top-tier signal in the Mission (0.83), second only to mole and pesto — and Sweetgreen has already proven the supply chain with the Miso Glazed Salmon Plate (active 2023-present), so this is a relaunch-adjacent format play, not net-new innovation.
novelty_check: Distinct from the existing Miso Glazed Salmon plate (which is a composed mezze-style plate) and the existing Crispy Rice Bowl (which is not salmon-anchored) — this fuses the two assets into a single bowl with nori sesame + napa cabbage as the headline texture/cuisine signals, all within the Japanese family.
confidence_reason: Miso glazed salmon has been on the menu since 2023, crispy rice is an established topping, and miso scores 0.83 locally. Every ingredient is active pantry and cuisine-coherent (all Japanese-family co-flavors).

dish_potential: An achiote-marinated chicken al pastor bowl with white rice, corn salsa, charred jalapeño ranch (existing), avocado, pickled onions, and tortilla chips — Sweetgreen's first real Mexican-family entry and a direct play for Mission lunch traffic that currently routes to taquerias. Pairs naturally with existing apple kimchi only via the KBBQ-taco fusion lineage, so we'd keep this bowl cuisine-pure (Mexican).
operational_lift: Medium lift — achiote paste is shelf-stable and the chicken marination program already exists (blackened, roasted, hot honey). Adding an al pastor marinade SKU is a recipe addition, not a new cookline.
comp_context: El Farolito and La Taqueria define al pastor in the Mission; Tacos Cala and Californios have shown the flavor scales to a craft-casual price point. Sweetgreen is currently invisible in this lane and ceding 100% of the al pastor lunch dollar.
